title: Spinal cord stimulation in 60 cases of intractable pain
journal_name: Journal of Neurology, Neurosurgery, and Psychiatry
study_question: What is the efficacy of spinal cord stimulation for intractable pain?
population_assessed: 60 patients with spinal cord stimulators for intractable pain
follow_up_duration: Two weeks to nine years (median 29 months)
outcome_measures: Made worse, no effect, modest benefit, significant benefit
inclusion_criteria: Intractable pain, unresponsive to other measures
answer_to_study_question: 70% of patients derived benefit (46.7% significant, 23.3% modest)
